name: Grid utilities
description: Modifiers and controls for elements rendered in 'grid' layout: track definition, item placement, and alignment. Apply 'display-grid' (from the layout manifest) to the container first, and space tracks with the 'gap' family (also in the layout manifest).

families.name: grid-template-columns
families.description: Defines the column tracks of a grid container.
guidance: grid-cols-{n} creates n equal, content-shrinkable columns via repeat(n, minmax(0, 1fr)); the minmax(0, …) lets tracks shrink below their content so long children can't blow out the layout.
grid-cols-subgrid adopts the parent grid's column tracks; verify browser support for your target baseline before relying on it.
Defining columns is purely visual — it does not change DOM order, so confirm the source order still reads logically for keyboard and screen reader users (WCAG 1.3.2).

families.name: grid-template-rows
families.description: Defines the row tracks of a grid container.
guidance: grid-rows-{n} creates n equal, content-shrinkable rows via repeat(n, minmax(0, 1fr)).
Explicit rows are most useful when you need a fixed structure; otherwise let content flow into implicit rows sized by grid-auto-rows.
grid-rows-subgrid adopts the parent grid's row tracks; verify browser support before relying on it.

families.name: grid-auto-flow
families.description: Controls how the auto-placement algorithm fills the grid and whether it back-fills holes (dense).
guidance: 'dense' packing pulls later items forward to fill earlier gaps, so visual order can diverge from DOM/reading/focus order. Use only where sequence carries no meaning, and tab through the result to confirm focus order still makes sense (WCAG 1.3.2, 2.4.3).
grid-flow-col switches auto-placement to fill down columns first instead of across rows.

families.name: grid-auto-columns
families.description: Sets the size of implicitly-created column tracks.
guidance: Applies to columns the grid creates beyond the explicit grid-template-columns definition.

families.name: grid-auto-rows
families.description: Sets the size of implicitly-created row tracks.
guidance: Applies to rows the grid creates beyond the explicit grid-template-rows definition — the common case when items flow into new rows.

families.name: grid-column
families.description: Sets how many columns a grid item spans, applied to the item itself.
guidance: col-span-full stretches an item across every column of the explicit grid (1 / -1).
Spanning is visual only — it does not move the item in the DOM. If placement implies a meaningful sequence, make sure the source order reflects it (WCAG 1.3.2).

families.name: grid-column-start
families.description: Sets the grid line where an item's column placement begins.
guidance: Line numbers are 1-based; line 13 is the trailing edge of a 12-column grid.
Explicit line placement reorders items visually without changing DOM order — verify the resulting reading and focus order still make sense (WCAG 1.3.2, 2.4.3).

families.name: grid-column-end
families.description: Sets the grid line where an item's column placement ends.
guidance: Pair with col-start-* to pin an item between two specific column lines.
Explicit line placement reorders items visually without changing DOM order — verify reading and focus order still make sense (WCAG 1.3.2, 2.4.3).

families.name: grid-row
families.description: Sets how many rows a grid item spans, applied to the item itself.
guidance: row-span-full stretches an item across every row of the explicit grid (1 / -1).
Spanning is visual only and does not change DOM order (WCAG 1.3.2).

families.name: grid-row-start
families.description: Sets the grid line where an item's row placement begins.
guidance: Explicit line placement reorders items visually without changing DOM order — verify reading and focus order still make sense (WCAG 1.3.2, 2.4.3).

families.name: grid-row-end
families.description: Sets the grid line where an item's row placement ends.
guidance: Pair with row-start-* to pin an item between two specific row lines.
Explicit line placement reorders items visually without changing DOM order — verify reading and focus order still make sense (WCAG 1.3.2, 2.4.3).

families.name: place-content
families.description: Shorthand aligning grid tracks on both axes at once (align-content + justify-content).
guidance: Declared before align-content so a more specific align-content-* class can override the block-axis half via the cascade.
Only has an effect when the grid's total track size is smaller than the container, leaving free space to distribute.

families.name: place-items
families.description: Shorthand aligning all grid items within their cells on both axes at once (align-items + justify-items).
guidance: Declared before justify-items so a more specific justify-items-* class can override the inline-axis half via the cascade.

families.name: place-self
families.description: Shorthand overriding the container's placement for a single grid item on both axes (align-self + justify-self).
guidance: Declared before justify-self so a more specific justify-self-* class can override the inline-axis half via the cascade.

families.name: align-content
families.description: Distributes grid rows along the block (cross) axis when the container has extra space.
guidance: Only has an effect when the grid's total row height is smaller than the container.
Also applies to multi-line flex containers, but this family lives with the grid utilities.

families.name: justify-items
families.description: Aligns all grid items within their cells along the inline (row) axis.

families.name: justify-self
families.description: Overrides the container's justify-items for a single grid item along the inline (row) axis.
